claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's start by looking at your situation. The first thing I need to know is about the child (or children) you'd like to claim Child Benefit for.

**How old is the child you want to claim for?**

Please let me know which applies:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children, we can go through each one - just let me know their ages and we'll assess eligibility for each child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh yes, I'd like to claim for my son Alex. He's 8 years old, so under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Lovely! Since Alex is under 16, he automatically meets the age requirement for Child Benefit. Let me move to the next step in the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check Alex's work status.

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

Since Alex is only 8, I'd expect he's not working - but I just need to confirm this to move forward. A simple "yes" or "no" will do!

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work at all - he's only 8! He's in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense - Alex is definitely not working, so that's a "yes" to the question (he's working less than 24 hours per week). Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving any benefits in his own right?**

This means benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit claimed by the child themselves. At 8 years old, this would be very unusual, but I need to confirm - is Alex free from receiving these types of benefits in his own name?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name - he's just a child!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Exactly, that's what I'd expect for an 8-year-old! So that's a "yes" - Alex is NOT receiving any benefits in his own right. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now I need to check about apprenticeships.

**Is Alex in an apprenticeship?**

At 8 years old in primary school, I'm confident the answer is no - but just to confirm: Alex is not currently doing any kind of apprenticeship, correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, of course not - he's only 8 and in primary school!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely! Alex is clearly not in an apprenticeship. Let me move on to the next part of the assessment, which will now look at your situation as the person claiming.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now we need to check your residency status. You can qualify through **any one** of the following paths:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You're a resident in the UK
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status with work** - You have pre-settled status and are working
4. **Pre-settled status as jobseeker** - You have pre-settled status and are actively looking for work
5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status and can support yourself financially
6. **Pre-settled status as a student** - You have pre-settled status and are studying
7. **Pre-settled status as family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. **Crown servant abroad** - You're living abroad as a Crown servant

**Do you normally live in the UK, or do any of the other situations apply to you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Living in Manchester means you normally reside in the UK, so you meet the residency requirements. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to confirm your responsibility for Alex. You can qualify through **any one** of these:

1. **Living with Alex** - Alex lives with you
2. **Contributing to Alex's upkeep** - You pay at least the Child Benefit amount towards his care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, etc.)
3. **Paying maintenance** - You make maintenance payments under a court order or agreement

**Does Alex live with you, or do you contribute to his care in another way?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Since Alex lives with you, you clearly meet the responsibility requirement. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good progress! Now I need to check about other potential claimants.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or planning to claim for him?**

For example, this could be another parent, a partner, or another family member. 

- If no one else is claiming, that's straightforward
- If someone else is claiming but you've agreed between yourselves that you should be the one to claim, that's also fine
- If there's a dispute about who should claim, HMRC would need to decide

What's your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that keeps things straightforward. Let's move on.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check Alex's living situation in a bit more detail.

**Where is Alex currently living?**

- **At home with you normally** (standard living arrangement)
- **In hospital**
- **In local authority care**
- **In a foster care arrangement**

Which applies to Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's at home with me - just a normal living situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Wonderful! A normal living situation at home - let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
We're almost there! Just one final question:

**Is the local council NOT paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, you're supporting Alex yourself without the local council contributing to his housing or upkeep costs. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - the council isn't paying anything towards Alex. I support him myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! That confirms everything I need. Let me now provide you with the outcome of your Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great news! 🎉

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## ✅ You ARE ELIGIBLE for Child Benefit for Alex!

Based on our assessment, you meet all the eligibility criteria:

**Why you're eligible:**
- ✓ Alex is under 16 years old
- ✓ Alex is not working 24+ hours per week
- ✓ Alex is not receiving benefits in his own right
- ✓ Alex is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship
- ✓ You normally live in the UK (Manchester)
- ✓ You are responsible for Alex (he lives with you)
- ✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- ✓ The local council is not contributing to Alex's upkeep

---

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** (or adoption certificate if applicable)
4. **Payments** are usually made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

⚠️ **Important:** If you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, be aware of the **High Income Child Benefit Charge** - you may still want to claim to protect your National Insurance record, but some or all of the benefit may need to be repaid through tax.

For more information and to apply, visit: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) He is under 16 years old (8 years old), so automatically qualifies on age; (2) He is not working 24+ hours per week; (3) He is not receiving benefits in his own right; (4) He is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship; (5) The claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester); (6) The claimant is responsible for Alex as he lives with them; (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex is in a normal living situation at home; (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. Alex qualifies as a child under 16 with no disqualifying factors, and the claimant meets all requirements as a UK resident who is responsible for Alex and supports him without council assistance.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) He is under 16 years old (8 years old), so automatically qualifies on age; (2) He is not working 24+ hours per week; (3) He is not receiving benefits in his own right; (4) He is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship; (5) The claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester); (6) The claimant is responsible for Alex as he lives with them; (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex is in a normal living situation at home; (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. Alex qualifies as a child under 16 with no disqualifying factors, and the claimant meets all requirements as a UK resident who is responsible for Alex and supports him without council assistance.
